entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Wearable fitness tracker used as data source for body composition and daily activity metrics

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine (sqlite3 module) used to store health and activity data locally

entities.name: daily_activity_summary
entities.context: Database table/schema tracking daily aggregate fitness metrics including steps, calories, active zone minutes

entities.name: Body Composition Tracking
entities.context: System for recording weight_kg, BMI, and fat_pct with upsert pattern

entities.name: Active Zone Minutes
entities.context: Fitbit metric broken into fat_burn, cardio, and peak sub-categories for activity intensity tracking

entities.name: upsert_daily_activity_summary
entities.context: Python function that inserts or updates a single day's Fitbit activity data into SQLite

entities.name: Health Data Pipeline
entities.context: Python-based pipeline ingesting Fitbit data into structured SQLite tables with raw JSON preservation

relationships.context: Fitbit is the primary data source feeding the daily_activity_summary table
Activity summaries are persisted in SQLite via upsert operations
Active Zone Minutes is a Fitbit-specific metric with fat_burn/cardio/peak breakdowns
Function uses sqlite3.Connection to write activity data
Pipeline ingests raw Fitbit API responses and structures them for local storage
Body composition metrics stored in SQLite with source attribution
